g.text: Understanding Yaw, Pitch, and Roll Physics

span.style: background-color: #3b82f6; color: white; padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-weight: 800; font-size: 14px; margin-right: 15px; letter-spacing: 0.5px;

div.p: Pitch is the up-and-down movement of the helicopter's nose. The pilot controls this by moving the Cyclic Stick forward or backward. This mechanically tilts the entire main rotor disc, directing the thrust vector forward (to accelerate) or backward (to slow down or reverse).
